question: Will the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25bp or more at the June 17, 2026 FOMC meeting?
reason: Federal Reserve officials favor rate hikes if inflation stays high, with no cuts expected in June.
evidence: Minutes from the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting reveal a majority of officials believe rate hikes may be necessary if inflation remains persistently above the 2% target. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/fed-rates-minutes-warsh?utm_source=openai))

The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at 4.25%–4.50% on May 7, 2026, indicating a cautious approach to monetary policy. ([investozora.com](https://investozora.com/fed-rate-hold-may-7-2026-fomc-decision/?utm_source=openai))
The next FOMC meeting is scheduled for June 16-17, 2026, with no indications of a rate cut at that time. ([federalreserve.gov](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy.htm?utm_source=openai))
